metric_value: Mature — approaching obsolescence as internet architectures emerge

metric_value: Rapidly emerging — vendors must support n-tier for enterprise credibility

metric_value: Reduced deployment costs vs. traditional methods

metric_value: Faster competitive-advantage upgrades than alternatives

metric_value: Partial — Java embedding planned but not yet fully implemented at time of study

metric_value: Java will become the dominant enterprise application language within 2-3 years

metric_name: Java language market dominance — actual outcome
metric_value: Java achieved dominant enterprise language status by 2000-2001; J2EE became the standard
notes: Java 2 Platform (J2SE 1.2) released 1998; J2EE launched 1999; dominant by 2000. Prediction verified.

metric_value: Tools without Java/internet support will become obsolete or lose enterprise relevance
notes: Explicit Aberdeen warning to enterprises evaluating toolsets

metric_value: PowerBuilder (Sybase) and SQLWindows (Gupta) declined sharply post-2000; 4GL tools displaced by J2EE frameworks
notes: PowerBuilder market share collapsed from dominant to niche by 2005; SQLWindows essentially abandoned

metric_value: VISION positioned to compete as top-tier enterprise development platform if Java roadmap executed
notes: Aberdeen's implicit prediction that VISION could achieve enterprise leadership

metric_value: Unify failed to achieve enterprise leadership; accounting fraud scandal 2000; CEO convicted 2003; delisted from Nasdaq; pivoted away from VISION
notes: Prediction did not materialize; Unify's trajectory reversed by internal governance failures
